title: Factorial Energy Road Tests Solid-State Cells With Stellantis As Stock Climbs
content:
Make better investment decisions with Simply Wall St's easy, visual tools that give you a competitive edge.

Factorial Energy has integrated its FEST solid-state battery cells into a Dodge Charger Daytona for on road testing in North America. The project is part of an ongoing partnership with Stellantis and marks the first time FEST cells have been installed in a Stellantis vehicle for automotive grade validation. This milestone moves Factorial from lab focused work toward real world testing, a key step before any potential commercial use of its technology.

For investors watching NasdaqCM:FAC, the move into on road testing comes as the stock trades at $15.33, with returns of 28.8% over the past week, 47.7% over the past month, and 48.5% year to date. The Stellantis integration puts Factorial Energy into a more visible position in the solid state battery space, tying its technology progress directly to a major global automaker.

From here, attention is likely to center on how the FEST equipped Charger Daytona performs in real world conditions and what additional milestones Factorial and Stellantis disclose. Any updates around durability, safety, and performance data, as well as potential timelines for broader testing, will be important markers for how the company’s technology story evolves.

0 things going right for Factorial Energy that this headline doesn't cover.

For Factorial Energy, installing FEST solid-state cells into a Dodge Charger Daytona is a shift from cell-level proof of concept to system-level validation in a real vehicle. The reported 375 Wh/kg energy density, 18-minute fast charge from 15% to 90%, and operation across a wide temperature range are already on the record from earlier testing, but packaging those cells into a Stellantis pack architecture and running them on public roads is what starts to test commercial readiness. This moves Factorial closer to where investors usually focus in the battery space, alongside peers such as QuantumScape, Solid Power, CATL, and LG Energy Solution, where the questions are less about lab metrics and more about integration with large automakers and eventual production pathways. For a company with a $3b valuation and no reported financial data yet, this kind of OEM-linked progress can be important for supporting the investment case around future revenue potential, especially given prior commentary about a limited cash runway and dilution risk if commercial contracts are slow to arrive.

Story Continues

The Risks and Rewards Investors Should Consider

⚠️ The company has not reported any financial data, which makes it harder for you to assess current revenue, cash burn, and profitability against a $3b valuation. ⚠️ A cash runway of 18 to 24 months, combined with no commercial revenue yet, points to a meaningful risk of future equity raises that could dilute existing shareholders if large production contracts are not secured in time. 🎁 The integration of FEST cells into a Stellantis development vehicle for road testing signals that Factorial’s technology has progressed beyond lab-scale, which may support discussions for deeper partnerships or production agreements if testing results are favorable. 🎁 Backing from major automakers such as Stellantis and Mercedes-Benz puts Factorial in a segment of the solid-state space where technical validation at scale can potentially attract further industrial and capital market support.

What To Watch Going Forward

From here, the key signposts are the outcomes of the Stellantis road-testing and calibration program and any follow-up communication on durability, safety, and charging performance in real-world use. Investors can watch for concrete milestones such as expanded test fleets, formal production-intent agreements, or joint investments in manufacturing capacity. Given the lack of reported financials and the flagged cash runway, updates around funding, potential non-dilutive capital, and any move toward revenue-generating contracts will also matter. Finally, Factorial’s share-price volatility over the past three months suggests sentiment can shift quickly, so new technical data or partnership announcements could play an outsized role in how the stock trades in the near term.
